enterprisePackage.answer1: When setting up the Enterprise Plan, you must either select an existing organization or create a new organization under which to subscribe.
enterprisePackage.answer2: Individual and Enterprise Plans operate as two completely separate systems. The Individual Plan is for personal use based on a single account, while the Enterprise Plan is organization-based and supports multi-user collaboration. The plans, points/credits, and data storage for both are completely isolated, non-shareable, and non-transferable. Even if the same account holds both plans, they function as two distinct environments with separate billing and management.
enterprisePackage.answer3: Yes, you can join or create multiple organizations and switch easily between different organizations and your individual account.
enterprisePackage.answer4: Organization points use a shared pool model. The Administrator can set a monthly usage limit for each member during every calendar month cycle.
enterprisePackage.question1: How do I subscribe to the Enterprise Plan?
enterprisePackage.question2: What is the difference between Individual and Enterprise Plans?
enterprisePackage.question3: Can a single user join multiple organizations?
enterprisePackage.question4: How are Enterprise Plan points allocated to members?
enterprisePackage.title: Enterprise Plans

payment.answer1: On the Stripe subscription payment page, you must use the "Select Currency" option to set the payment currency to "Hong Kong Dollar (HKD).". Once set, you will be able to select WeChat Pay or Alipay to complete your subscription payment.
payment.answer2: If your bank blocked your credit card payment, please re-verify that the card number, expiration date, CVC code, and billing address (including the postal code) are all entered correctly. Also, ensure there are sufficient available funds on the card. If the issue persists, please contact your respective bank or card issuer for assistance.
payment.answer3: You can contact us via the "Feedback" function on the page. Please provide the order details you wish to refund (the order number can be found on your payment platform account) and the reason for the refund request. If your order meets our refund criteria, the refunded amount will be returned to your original payment account within 1 to 15 business days.
payment.question1: How can I pay for my subscription using WeChat Pay or Alipay?
payment.question2: Why was my credit card payment declined?
payment.question3: How do I request a refund?
payment.title: Payment & Refunds

personalPackage.answer1: You can subscribe by visiting the "Subscription" page and selecting the plan you wish to purchase. Upon upgrading, you will gain access to all the benefits of that plan.
personalPackage.answer10: Purchase on Mar 31 → Renews on May 1
personalPackage.answer11: Annual plans are calculated based on a fixed 372-day cycle (12 x 31 days). The full billing cycle runs for 372 days starting from the date of purchase. For example:
personalPackage.answer12: Purchase on June 15, 2024 → Billing cycle is June 15, 2024 – June 22, 2025 (372 days)
personalPackage.answer13: Purchase on Feb 29, 2024 → Billing cycle is Feb 29, 2024 – Mar 7, 2025 (372 days)
personalPackage.answer14: Please refer to our current pricing scheme for specific details on annual plan discounts.
personalPackage.answer15: If you subscribe to both the monthly and annual versions of the same plan tier, they will be activated sequentially based on the time of subscription. Specifically, if you subscribe to the monthly plan first, followed by the annual plan, the monthly plan benefits will be active first, and the annual plan benefits will take effect immediately after the monthly subscription expires.
personalPackage.answer16: We apologize, but we do not currently support plan transfers.
personalPackage.answer2: You can upgrade your plan directly on the page. If you are already on a plan and upgrade to a higher-tier subscription, the highest-tier plan will take effect immediately. The remaining term of your original (lower-tier) plan will be paused and will resume only after the higher-tier plan expires. Please note: Points already issued from your original plan remain in your account, but those specific points will not be re-issued when the original plan resumes for its next cycle.
personalPackage.answer3: Monthly plans are calculated based on a fixed 31-day cycle. Starting from the purchase date, every 31 days constitutes one full billing cycle. For example:
personalPackage.answer4: Purchase on Jan 15 → Billing cycle is Jan 15 – Feb 15 (31 days)
personalPackage.answer5: Purchase on Mar 31 → Billing cycle is Mar 31 – May 1 (31 days)
personalPackage.answer6: The first purchase is charged the full cycle rate, covering one complete 31-day period starting from the date of purchase.
personalPackage.answer7: The renewal date is the 31st day following the purchase date. For example:
personalPackage.answer8: Purchase on Jan 15 → Renews on Feb 15
personalPackage.answer9: Purchase on Feb 28 → Renews on Mar 31
personalPackage.question1: How do I subscribe to a plan?
personalPackage.question2: How do I upgrade my plan?
personalPackage.question3: How is the billing cycle for monthly plans calculated??
personalPackage.question4: If I buy a monthly plan mid-month, how is the fee calculated?
personalPackage.question5: How is the renewal date for monthly plans determined?
personalPackage.question6: How is the billing cycle for annual plans calculated?
personalPackage.question7: What discounts do annual plans offer compared to monthly plans?
personalPackage.question8: How is the validity period calculated if I subscribe to both monthly and annual plans simultaneously?
personalPackage.question9: Can I transfer my plan to another account?
personalPackage.title: Individual Plans

points.answer1: Points for annual plans are issued in 31-day installments. Each batch of gifted points is only valid for 31 days. They will expire automatically at the end of the cycle and do not roll over.
points.answer10: June 7 (Next Year): Receive 12th cycle points (+341 days)
points.answer11: The system usually issues points automatically at your subscription time. Delays may occur due to system maintenance, but points will be credited before 24:00 (midnight) on that day. If you have not received points after 24 hours, please contact us.
points.answer13: 1. Confirm that your subscription is active.
points.answer14: 2. Check your point transaction history for any error messages.
points.answer15: 3. If points are still missing after 24 hours, please contact us for assistance.
points.answer16: Unused points will automatically expire and be cleared after the 31-day cycle; they do not roll over.
points.answer17: You can purchase "Point Packs" from the Top-Up page. These purchased points will have a 372-day validity period. Additionally, you can earn points by participating in various activities and promotions launched by our platform.
points.answer18: The system automatically determines the usage priority based on the points' expiration date. Points that are set to expire the soonest will always be used first.
points.answer2: Points are issued according to the following schedule:
points.answer3: First Cycle: Points for the first cycle are added immediately upon successful purchase.
points.answer4: Following 11 Cycles: Points for the current period are added every 31 days on your subscription anniversary time.
points.question1: If I subscribe to an annual plan, are points issued at once or periodically?
points.question2: When exactly do the points for an annual plan arrive?
points.question3: What is the exact time points are credited?
points.question4: What should I do if my points are not credited on time?
points.question5: What happens if I don't use all my cycle's points?
points.question6: How can I get more points?
points.question7: What is the usage priority between plan points and purchased points?
points.title: Points Issuance & Usage

teamPackage.answer1: When setting up a Team Plan, you must either select an existing team organization you have created or establish a new team organization under which to subscribe.
teamPackage.answer2: The Individual and Team Plans are two entirely separate systems. The Individual Plan is tied to a single user account for personal use, while the Team Plan is based on a team organization and supports multi-user collaboration. Their plans, points, and data storage are completely isolated, non-shareable, and non-transferable. Even if the same user account holds both plans, they function as two independent usage environments, each with separate billing and management.
teamPackage.answer3: Yes, you can join or create multiple team organizations and switch easily between different teams and your individual account.
teamPackage.answer4: Team points operate on a shared pool model. The Administrator can set and assign a usage cap for each member during every calendar month cycle.
teamPackage.question1: How do I subscribe to a Team Plan?
teamPackage.question2: What is the difference between the Individual and Team Plans?
teamPackage.question3: Can a single user join multiple teams?
teamPackage.question4: How are Team Plan points allocated to members?
teamPackage.title: Team Plans

bonusPointsModal.contactAdminContent: Team subscription renewal or recharge points please contact the team creator or other team administrators for recharge.
bonusPointsModal.continueSubscriptionTip1: Your current package is currently active.
bonusPointsModal.continueSubscriptionTip2: When your points are insufficient, you can directly recharge points separately for use.
bonusPointsModal.continueSubscriptionTip3: If you need to renew your package or subscribe to a new one, the renewed or new package will take effect automatically after the current package expires (without affecting the use of the current package).
bonusPointsModal.freeUnlimitedModelTip: Disclaimer: Free unlimited models are only applicable to $t(common:platform.name) and Chat scenarios. Features such as MagiClaw and process orchestration are not covered by this benefit.
bonusPointsModal.freeUnlimitedModelsTip: Free unlimited models are available for $t(common:platform.name). During task execution, capabilities such as web search, image analysis and generation, document parsing will still incur a small amount of points. Usage of large models for non-$t(common:platform.name) functions (e.g., process orchestration, API calls, etc.) will be billed according to normal consumption.
bonusPointsModal.iOSPaymentDelayTip: iOS payment may have a 30-second to 1-minute delay, please wait patiently.

modelConsumption.inputTokensDescription: nput / Million Tokens: The cost (in credits/points) required per one million tokens of input—referring to the instructions, prompts, or data you send to the AI agent.
modelConsumption.modalityDescription: Modality: The type of data (e.g., text, image, audio, video) that the AI model is capable of processing or generating.
modelConsumption.modelConsumptionDisclaimer: Model availability is subject to change as we continuously upgrade our product features and underlying architecture. Please refer to the current usage environment for the most up-to-date status.
modelConsumption.outputTokensDescription: Output / Million Tokens: The cost (in credits/points) required per one million tokens of output—referring to the response, answer, or result generated and returned to you by the AI agent.
modelConsumption.singleCallDescription: Single Invocation: The fixed number of credits/points consumed for initiating one complete request to the model, which includes sending the input and receiving the corresponding output.
